title: Documentation: Examples after the first one are indented inappropriately
body:
### Issue Description

Look at the `podman push -h` output:

```
Push an image to a specified destination
[...]
Usage:
  podman push [options] IMAGE [DESTINATION]

Examples:
  podman push imageID docker://registry.example.com/repository:tag
                podman push imageID oci-archive:/path/to/layout:image:tag

Options:
```

Note how the second example is way off to the right. Source code: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/509e44a00599e11432a08e7d1bc680c61c98ff30/cmd/podman/images/push.go#L44-L45

```go
	// Command: podman push
	pushCmd = &cobra.Command{
		Use:               "push [options] IMAGE [DESTINATION]",
		// [...]
		Example: `podman push imageID docker://registry.example.com/repository:tag
		podman push imageID oci-archive:/path/to/layout:image:tag`,
	}
```

I suppose, the backticks string were meant to strip leading whitespaces, but they never actually behaved that way in Go?

**This issue is present in many other commands, not just `push`!**

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman push -h`

### Describe the results you received

Second example is indented.

### Describe the results you expected

Second example should not be indented.

### podman info output

```yaml
version:
  APIVersion: 5.5.2
  Built: 1754604929
  BuiltTime: Fri Aug  8 01:15:29 2025
  GitCommit: e7d8226745ba07a64b7176a7f128e4ef53225a0e
  GoVersion: go1.24.5
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 5.5.2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Podman seems to override the default cobra template and adds two spaces before `{{Examples}}` but that means subsequent newlines must be prefixed with explicit 2-space prefixes for every command.

Backticks would render the text verbatim so in this case, the prefix is a tab instead of spaces, and hence the rendering mismatch. There seem to be a few other commands that also have this problem. Would you like to raise a PR fixing this?
I don't know about cobra and template, but it sounds like a thing where all these issues can be fixed in one place.

Rather than fixing each command individually, I added a centralized `indentExamples` template function that normalizes all Example strings to exactly 2-space indentation, regardless of how the raw string literal is indented in source code. The function is registered via cobra.AddTemplateFunc and piped in the usage template ({{.Example | indentExamples}}).

This also means future contributors don't need to worry about matching indentation in their backtick strings, the template function handles it uniformly.
